ks1.objective: Hit the ball, then run together as a team around the cones before the fielders pass the ball over and under to the back.
successCriteria: Holds the bat with two hands
Joins the back of the team line for the run
Stays in line as the team runs together

lks2.objective: Develop team fielding — field cleanly, form a line, pass over and under to limit the batting team's runs.
successCriteria: Batting team stays in sync as a 'caterpillar'

equipment.item: Cones to mark the run circuit

space.description: A wicket at one end with a run circuit of cones. Field area opens out in front. Team lines up behind the batter.

steps: Divide the class into small teams (max 5) — fielding and batting team.
Once the batter has struck the ball they join the back of the team line, who then run around the cones as a team.
The fielding team fields the ball, then forms a line and passes the ball over and under from front to back.
The batting team gets a point for every batter safely back home.
Swap roles after every batter has had a turn.
questions: Fielders, come up with a plan to retrieve the balls as quickly as possible.
How do you field a ground ball?
Why should you back up your team-mate in the field?

step.space: Make the caterpillar longer to run around
step.task: Fielders have to throw to each other instead of standing in a line

errors.fix: Cue 'low and ready — bend the knees'

errors.issue: One hand when two would secure it

errors.fix: Insist on a 'mine!' before every play
